reason: The text describes a situation where David sinned by numbering the people of Israel, which displeased God and led to a great disaster in the form of a pestilence that resulted in the death of seventy thousand men. Following this great destruction, there is a meaningful starting over as David seeks forgiveness and makes offerings to God to stay the plague and appease the Lord. The building of a new altar and the offering of sacrifices symbolize a fresh beginning after the disaster.
